prediction.reasoning: This prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, commonly used for stock price forecasting. The model uses the current price ($106.05), an estimated annualized volatility (sigma) of 25%, and an estimated annualized drift (mu) of 9% (representing a long-term average return) over a 20-day period (approximately 0.079 years). The point estimate represents the median price from this log-normal distribution. The confidence intervals are calculated using standard deviations derived from the estimated volatility over the 20-day horizon. It's crucial to note that stock market predictions are inherently uncertain and these are statistical probabilities, not guarantees. Actual prices can deviate significantly due to unforeseen market events, company-specific news, and broader economic factors not captured by this statistical model.
